notes:
so got the game to launch, by using winetricks WINEPREFIX="[location of steam]/steamapps/compatdata/292120/pfx" winetricks
ERASING the wine prefix... and then running winetricks again to install dotnet4...
I had a fair amount of issue with actually getting into the game. most "complex" menus would lock controller input out. (e.g the start new game "easy" and "normal" confirmation menu, or keyboard/controller config)
turns out microsoft released a patch for this game for exactly that.
https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/insider/forum/all/final-fantasy-xiii-input-freezes-on-saveload-menu/684231d1-de4e-47b4-be22-87c23eb198ed
I disconnected the ps3 controller i use (xbox360) and instantly stopped happening.
so my guess is that issue.
i'll update when i've played for a while.
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes:
i'm continuing my previous post...
i've now gained access to Snow, after two crashes, and about 10 minutes of unneccessary lockups/loading glitches. 
it's almost like the game's not clearing it's ram or vram. you get about 10-20 minutes of gameplay, then crash. 
the loadtime glitch however is really annoying, since i've installed this on a ssd that the game DOES load from almost instantly, then other times, it'll take minutes. i've seen this happen in the same area before and after a crash, different speeds of course.
the longer the playtime, the more likely you'll slow down, and the more likely it'll "freeze" doing something. for example the fight animation as you "collide" with an enemy force.
that can be seamlessly perfect. or it can be an uncomfortable 5 minutes as the game just leaves the character standing in "attack" position.
add in the broken controller support (and let's face it, this game is built for controller really)
and i'd have to say the game is fundamentally broken. sure it operates, and the game itself seems to be fine, but whatever is causing this crashing means you can't really get lost in the game. and you'll ALWAYS be running forwards or backwards to a save after EVERY battle, since there is a good chance it'll crash on the next one...
i'm going to try installing it's stuff manually and see how it runs...
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes:
continuing (third post now), that was a BAD move. looks like i've broken something in a big way, tried installing XACT (as requested by a wine post), and that is my suspicion of culprit.
the game is no longer able to start at all (it can't allocate enough ram), a purge of either FFXIII, the proton prefix, or both does not stop this error now.
game uninstalled for now. since apparently i can't keep testing anyway. 
i'm guessing it's saved something in steam cloud, since it's currently removed, i can't test this theory, (and it'll take too much SSD space and time to get back for a maybe.)

in summary, when it was running, the game was hard to play, controllers could NOT be connected at all if you wanted to be able to play (physical disconnection),
it did not run for long before crashing, and had evidence of some sort of failure to manage it's memory properly (slower and slower loading, glitches that indicate bad loading)
i'm confident that with a little time, square enix could fix this easily enough, or with more time, steam/wine could themselves.
barring this, rare texture glitches sometimes occur (e.g, sazh's afro would sometimes
partially render through his face at the "right" camera angle in cutscenes, in this case a close up of the side of his face on the train scene).
but beyond that, i never noticied anything actually "wrong" with the game, the controls operated as expected in relation to context, sound was decent enough, nothing odd.
and video came through as good as expected. (barring the mentioned layering glitches)
but considering that i can now no longer start the game at all, this entry is borked,
my first post was benefit of the doubt. so had silver. and honestly, bronze is a little too much credit for the level of use from the second attempt.
sorry it's so long.
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes:
well i was wrong, had not uninstalled the game like i thought. I had to disable steam cloud sync, as it seems to be re-applying my damaged install (probably just dumping all changes as the save)
proceded to wipe the compat data for the game.
i'm not 100% sure on exactly HOW i get everything installed, as it's a fair amount of just trying till it works.
I think however the order is to install the game and run it once, it will fail.
use winetricks to PURGE it's data entirely (mostly, running the game is just for the correct directory setup)
use winetricks to install dotnet 4.0 and 4.5.2, also vcruntime 2010 and 2012... finally install XACT (is it actually needed, a old wine post said yes, so it seems a good idea)
run the game again. steam will attempt to install the redistributable files again.
(if not, keep trying to start the game, it should eventually try again)
do NOT stop this install, it will take ages to FAIL near the end. this is actually a good thing.
once more, the game FAILS to start.
goto winetricks again, and attempt to install dotnet 4.5.2 and vcruntime 2012 again, this will give you "repair" options.
so repair them.
we are *NOT* completely out of the woods yet...
DISCONNECT any GAMEPAD/CONTROLLER devices, THEN "start" the game in steam,
so we have the launcher. run the game BELOW your normal settings.
I go from 1080p to 720p (my r9 270 windforce oc 2x is fairly comparible to a gtx 1050, both are 2gb cards though)
otherwise although the game will start, your gameplay will be short.
the game *WILL* start without disabling ESYNC, but this will make the game take longer and longer to load in short order... like using a bad disk on a console.
disabling esync also cuts the fps down rather a lot, using the wined3d switch
recovers a portion of that. hence the drop from 1080p to 720p

use EVERY save point, and often. i'm averaging anywhere from 5 to 20 fights before the game crashes out. this is a VIDEORAM issue i think, since i've seen graphic artifact glitching in the fal'cie corridor (in the shadow of l'cie) (the floor had a very nice "shine" that was garbage text, very close looked bad, but distant... it looked amazing... about 5 seconds later the game crashed.
reloaded from the SAME save point. it's gone.

*************
* Problems *
************ 
Video - vram not clearing? (source of regular but erratic game crash?)
           - stuttering occurs erratically, often from camera panning, sometimes not...
           - performance has decreased due to disabled Esync...
controller - see other post for website link, basically the x360 controller has a WINDOWS patch. maybe too much effort to make this work... (steam controller hacky workaround though?)

Esync - this needs to be fixed, although the game itself runs better without esync, the rendering system seems to run MUCH better with esync. 

in summary, we now know how to OPERATE the game, where the system is most likely misbehaving. and this should make it much easier for square or valve to fix.

hopefully i've covered everything this time. unless i figure out a better fix, or an update happens (game or proton), I don't have a reason to keep posting...

good luck
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes:
I tested pretty much everything I could find. wine64 (page fault since the game is 32bit) protontricks, winetricks,  to try and get dotnet40 installed...
Then I tried creating a 32 bit wineprefix (the game is 32bit, whoever tries should mind),  and using winetricks to install the necessary dlls (which I read directly from FFXIII script), plus XACT and some more... to no avail whatsoever. Proton would not detect these dlls were already installed and would try to download and install them...
I also tried, with all configurations, to launch the launcher in common/FINAL FANTASYXIII/, which is launched but never runs the game. And to launch the application binary in common/FINAL FANTASY XIII/prog/win/bin/ffxiiiimg.exe.
With a 64bit prefix, I get square enix's opening screen and some Direct 8 message in messed font, then it closes due to the page fault (game is 32bit). When trying a direct launch with a 32bit prefix, the game does not launch whatsoever.

Guess I'll have to wait until either Proton is more mature, or wine, or both. To whoever was able to get this cursed thing to run, kudos to you.

ps: some weird "net Runtime optimization service is started and paused" cmdline screen sometimes showed up with the 32bit prefix when trying to launch from steam, looping until just "The .NET RunTime Optimization Service is running". Then the game  launching tab would eventually change to "Preparing to Launch...", forever, and never launch at all.
protonVersion: 3.7-2 (or so it seems from proton script)
protonVersionManual: 3.7-2 (or so it seems from proton script)
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes: Ran "protontricks 292120 net40", but when trying to start the game it still wants to install dotnet. It doesn't just error out once and then give up, like a previous gold report suggests, but will retry installing on the next start attempt.
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes: After installing Protontricks through pip, getting the gui for it up, erasing everything (or whatever the gui called the last option), and installing dotnet40, I got it to run.  If you set the MSAA too high, it'll crash on every screen resolution setting other than 720p with windowed.  I set MSAA at 2x and fullscreen and windowered at 1080 works fine.  The framerate constantly goes from 30 to 60.  Other than restarting the game because I couldn't complete the second Eidolon fight (which apparently was also a Windows problem), the only other real issue was that the hair is flat and blocky and the eyelashes looked like the characters put too much eyeliner on.
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes: The game runs out of the box. The only reason i don't rate this game gold is because of the blocky eyelashes/hair. This is an issue that can happen on windows too if AA is disabled. Overriding application settings and forcing AA in the nvidia control center didn't fix this. Wine D3d helped with the micro stutter. However that is on windows too and isn't too dramatic. other than that the game runs perfect.

title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes: Ran this out of the box on Proton 4.2-5. Game ran without crashing on default settings until I got to chapter 8 of the storyline. At the beginning of chapter 8 there is a cutscene that consistently crashed the game. After some research, I learned that switching into windowed mode would allow the video to play without crashing the game. After the scene played out, I switched back into full screen mode and continued playing without any more crashes. Throughout my 233 hour playthrough I used an Xbox 360 usb controller. While this worked in general, every time a window popped up, I would have to hit the top left lower trigger button several (6-10 times) to get the window to unstick? This was most common when interacting with all the save point menus. Annoying perhaps, took a bit of research to figure out what was going on, but overall wasn't a crippling issue.
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes:
With Fullscreen mode, I experienced a lot of crashes - when loading the game, at certain cutscenes, randomly. The framerate was also pretty bad at 1440p - around 15-30, though I did play through a particularly busy area in this stint (another user mentioned that Chapter 8 had particular issues). In general, it didn't go well.

Unfortunately windowed mode only lets you go up to 1080p, so I ended up turning my monitor's resolution down to get pseudo-fullscreen. The experience was much better after this - no more crashes, generally good performance - usually 50-60FPS (I think it's capped at 60) with the odd stutter here and there. Oh, and don't try setting shadow resolution above 2048x2048, and anti-aliasing doesn't work, so leave it at 2x.

Using a PS4 controller was a bit problematic. The game doesn't support PS4 controllers natively - this seems to be an issue on Windows too. I imagine a 360/xinput controller might work out of the box, but I haven't tried. Steam does a good job of emulating keyboard inputs on the controller though, the default mappings were ok, after a few tweaks (add WASD controls and num pad to the analog sticks) it was perfect.

One particularly weird glitch that I only solved accidentally - if I have a controller connected when I start the game, then go to load a save, the game ignores all input, so I can't load the save. If I start the game with the controller disconnected, go through the menus with the keyboard to load the save, it works - the controller works when connected after that point.

All in all, a bit of a hassle to get working reliably, but if you go with windowed mode and tweak the controls, you shouldn't have too many issues.

title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes: So after reading about some of the issues surrounding the windows port of FF13, it seems that it was poorly handled and that the stuttering had to do with how they didn't properly adjust the engine to x86 architecture (compared to cell architecture from the PS3). Supposedly all the calls are done in a single thread, ignoring multi- threaded processors, hence the stutter. With that in mind I decided to enable D9vk because Vulkan was made for these type of situations (low overhead for CPU constrained games), this seems to solve all the stuttering for me. Some other things which I noticed was that the menu's respond faster (it would be interesting to compare Windows fps with Linux due to this poor port, it might be that Linux would out perform Windows here).
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes: Works fine, little stutter on full screen, I am stuck on 20-25 FPS but that might be my own hardware limiting me. works well with the new D9VK
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes:
for this game you need a 32bit prefix, is easy ..
in Set Launch Option put:
WINEARCH=win32 %command%

That is to have a 32-bit prefix, it works with any game that requires it.
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes: connecting an x-box controller generates big delays after context switches (i.e. going from start menu to load a game, or from battle to loot screen). Playing with a keyboard is smoother but generally uncomfortable for this type of lazy console port.
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes:
Run perfect with launch options "WINEARCH=win32 PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_USE_D9VK=1  %command%" since Proton version 4.11-7. I play with keyboard and mouse.
You can minimize the game by creating a virtual desktop in winecfg.
title: FINAL FANTASY® XIII

notes:
Got the game into a perfect state. Using d9vk, but have seen reports that using Wine d9dx is OK. playing with a controller needed some working around, at the end by installing dinput8 I was able to get the game to recognize the controller and kept working even when the game manus would appear. Using fsync. My final lunch arguments where WINEARCH=win32 PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_LOG=1 DXVK_HUD=1 %command%
